rulings.Case Title: VERNON FARQUHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT REDWOOD CITY ELECTRIC, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE FORM OF THE DEMURRER FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1320. C.C.P. SECTION 340.2 IS APPLICABLE TO ASBESTOS ACTIONS AND THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUFFERED A DISABILITY. THE SUPREME COURT HAS GRANTED REVIEW IN MITCHELL V. ASBESTO CORP. (2000) 62 CAL. APP. 4TH 200. THE REMAINDER OF DEFENDANT'S CONTENTIONS ARE BEST RESOLVED THROUGH DISCOVERY. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III, LLC, A et al VS. MATTHEW E. CRISP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Allegations Of Second Amended Cross-Complaint Filed By Deft Matthew E. Crisp; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion To Strike Allegations Of Second Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 9. PLAINTIFF VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY II, L.L.C., JOANNA REES-GALLANTER, VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III L.L.C. AND JOHN HAMM'S Motion To Strike Allegations Of Second Amended Cross-Complaint Filed By DEFENDANT Matthew E. Crisp IS CONTINUED TO MAY 18, 2007. ON THAT DATE, COURT, ON ITS OWN MOTION, WILL CONTINUE THE MATTER TO A DIFFERENT AGREED UPON HEARING DATE. PARTIES ARE TO SELECT AN AGREED UPON DATE FROM THE FOLLOWING DATES FOR THE HEARING: MAY 21, 22, 23 AND JUNE 5, 6, & 7, 2007. PARTIES ARE TO INFORM THE COURT OF THE AGREED UPON DATE PRIOR TO MAY 18, 2007. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY ZUKOWSKI VS. THE GRANADA, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT THE GRANADA, INC., MICHAEL LIEBERMAN AND IRENE LIEBERMAN'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT: DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS, SPECIFICALLY IN PARAGRAPHS EIGHT AND TWENTY-FIVE, THAT ARE SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A BASIS THAT RICHARD LIEBERMAN HAD NO INTENTION OF PERFORMING THE PROMISES ALLEGEDLY MADE. TARMANN V STATE FARM (1991) 2 CAL. APP. 4TH, 153, 158. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE UNDER TARMANN, THE CAUSE OF ACTION CANNOT BE PREMISED ON PREDICTIONS OR PROMISES AS TO FUTURE EVENTS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD VINSON VS. COMMUNITY COLLEGE BOARD OF SAN FRANCISCO., CITY
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 10. PLAINTIFF HAROLD VINSON'S Motion For Relief From Order THE MOTIONS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P. SECTION 1008 BECAUSE THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING OF NEW FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW THAT WARRANT RECONSIDERATION. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. SECTION 473(B). =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: AUBREY HUNTER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Disqualify Attorney Nicholas Aloia And The Law Firm Of Haapala, Altura, Thompson Abern, Llp From Representing Defendant Pacific Flooring Supply, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 5. PLAINTIFF AUBREY HUNTER'S MOTION To Disqualify Attorney Nicholas Aloia And The Law Firm Of Haapala, Altura, Thompson, Abern, L.L.P. From Representing Defendant Pacific Flooring Supply, Inc. IS DENIED BASED ON ADAMS V. AEROJET-GENERAL CORP. (2001) 86 CAL. APP. 4TH 1324, 1340. ALOIA HAS ESTABLISHED THAT HE HAD NO CONTACT WITH AND ACQUIRED NO INFORMATION RELATED TO PLAINTIFF OR HIS CASE. THE MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO REBUT THE ALOIA DECLARATION. IN FACT, THE MOVING PAPERS ARE CONSISTENT WITH THE ALOIA DECLARATION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 7. PETITIONER ALAN GEDDES' PETITION To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Application To Appoint Director(S); Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 11, 2007, LINE 8. PETITIONER NORMAN VORTIGY'S Motion For Hearing On Application To Appoint Director(S) IS DENIED. THERE APPEAR TO BE MATERIAL DISPUTES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER MR. VORTIGY IS A "MEMBER" OF THE INSTITUTE. IT ALSO IS UNCLEAR FROM THE RECORD WHAT IS REQUIRED FOR ONE TO BECOME A MEMBER. AT THE SAME TIME, IT APPEARS THAT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES CONSISTS ONLY OF THREE MEMBERS IN APPARENT VIOLATION OF THE BY-LAWS. THE MATTERS RAISED BY PETITIONER ARE BEST ADDRESSED BY THE OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. =(302/PJM/KD)
